test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__bridge_blocto_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.bridge_blocto
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__event_attributes_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.event_attributes
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__transactions_blocto_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.transactions_blocto
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__swaps_single_trade_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.swaps_single_trade
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__nft_transactions_secondary_market_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.nft_transactions_secondary_market
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.prices_swaps
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__events_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.events
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__events_final_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.events_final
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__blocks_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.blocks
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__transactions_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.transactions
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__nft_topshot_sales_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.nft_topshot_sales
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_silver__bridge_celer_block_timestamp__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.silver.bridge_celer
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(block_timestamp as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_core__fact_bridge_transactions_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.core.fact_bridge_transactions
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_core__fact_nft_sales_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.core.fact_nft_sales
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

    TX_ID as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

test.flow_models.tx_gaps_core__fact_tran.name: tx_gaps_core__fact_transactions_block_height__TX_ID__tx_count
test.flow_models.tx_gaps_core__fact_tran.compiled_sql:
WITH block_base AS (
    SELECT
        block_height,
        tx_count
    FROM
        FLOW.silver.blocks
),
model_name AS (
    SELECT
        block_height,
        COUNT(
            DISTINCT TX_ID
        ) AS model_tx_count
    FROM
        (select * from FLOW.core.fact_transactions where BLOCK_TIMESTAMP < CURRENT_DATE) dbt_subquery
    GROUP BY
        block_height
)
SELECT
    block_base.block_height,
    tx_count,
    model_name.block_height,
    model_tx_count
FROM
    block_base
    LEFT JOIN model_name
    ON block_base.block_height = model_name.block_height
WHERE
    tx_count <> model_tx_count

test.flow_models.not_null_core__fact_tra.name: not_null_core__fact_transactions_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
test.flow_models.not_null_core__fact_tra.compiled_sql:
select BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
from FLOW.core.fact_transactions
where BLOCK_TIMESTAMP is null

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_core__fact_transactions_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.core.fact_transactions
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_core__fact_blocks_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.core.fact_blocks
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.sequence_gaps_core__fac.name: sequence_gaps_core__fact_blocks_block_height
test.flow_models.sequence_gaps_core__fac.compiled_sql:
WITH source AS (
    SELECT
        
        block_height,
        LAG(
            block_height,
            1
        ) over (
            
            ORDER BY
                block_height ASC
        ) AS prev_block_height
    FROM
        (select * from FLOW.core.fact_blocks where BLOCK_TIMESTAMP < CURRENT_DATE) dbt_subquery
)
SELECT
    
    prev_block_height,
    block_height,
    block_height - prev_block_height
    - 1 AS gap
FROM
    source
WHERE
    block_height - prev_block_height <> 1
ORDER BY
    gap DESC

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_core__fact_swaps_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.core.fact_swaps
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.name: dbt_expectations_expect_row_values_to_have_recent_data_core__fact_events_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP__day__1
test.flow_models.dbt_expectations_expect.compiled_sql:
with max_recency as (

    select max(cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
)) as max_timestamp
    from
        FLOW.core.fact_events
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast(BLOCK_TIMESTAMP as 
    timestamp_ntz
) <= cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        
)
select
    *
from
    max_recency
where
    -- if the row_condition excludes all rows, we need to compare against a default date
    -- to avoid false negatives
    coalesce(max_timestamp, cast('1970-01-01' as 
    timestamp_ntz
))
        <
        cast(

    dateadd(
        day,
        -1,
        cast(convert_timezone('UTC', 'GMT', 
    current_timestamp::
    timestamp_ntz

) as 
    timestamp_ntz
)
        )

 as 
    timestamp_ntz
)

macro.dbt_snowflake.snowflake__get_catal.name: snowflake__get_catalog
macro.dbt_snowflake.snowflake__get_catal.macro_sql:
{% macro snowflake__get_catalog(information_schema, schemas) -%}
  {% set query %}
      with tables as (

          select
              table_catalog as "table_database",
              table_schema as "table_schema",
              table_name as "table_name",
              table_type as "table_type",
              comment as "table_comment",

              -- note: this is the _role_ that owns the table
              table_owner as "table_owner",

              'Clustering Key' as "stats:clustering_key:label",
              clustering_key as "stats:clustering_key:value",
              'The key used to cluster this table' as "stats:clustering_key:description",
              (clustering_key is not null) as "stats:clustering_key:include",

              'Row Count' as "stats:row_count:label",
              row_count as "stats:row_count:value",
              'An approximate count of rows in this table' as "stats:row_count:description",
              (row_count is not null) as "stats:row_count:include",

              'Approximate Size' as "stats:bytes:label",
              bytes as "stats:bytes:value",
              'Approximate size of the table as reported by Snowflake' as "stats:bytes:description",
              (bytes is not null) as "stats:bytes:include",

              'Last Modified' as "stats:last_modified:label",
              to_varchar(convert_timezone('UTC', last_altered), 'yyyy-mm-dd HH24:MI'||'UTC') as "stats:last_modified:value",
              'The timestamp for last update/change' as "stats:last_modified:description",
              (last_altered is not null and table_type='BASE TABLE') as "stats:last_modified:include"

          from {{ information_schema }}.tables

      ),

      columns as (

          select
              table_catalog as "table_database",
              table_schema as "table_schema",
              table_name as "table_name",

              column_name as "column_name",
              ordinal_position as "column_index",
              data_type as "column_type",
              comment as "column_comment"

          from {{ information_schema }}.columns
      )

      select *
      from tables
      join columns using ("table_database", "table_schema", "table_name")
      where (
        {%- for schema in schemas -%}
          upper("table_schema") = upper('{{ schema }}'){%- if not loop.last %} or {% endif -%}
        {%- endfor -%}
      )
      order by "column_index"
    {%- endset -%}

  {{ return(run_query(query)) }}

{%- endmacro %}

macro.dbt.default__snapshot_staging_tabl.name: default__snapshot_staging_table
macro.dbt.default__snapshot_staging_tabl.macro_sql:
{% macro default__snapshot_staging_table(strategy, source_sql, target_relation) -%}

    with snapshot_query as (

        {{ source_sql }}

    ),

    snapshotted_data as (

        select *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key

        from {{ target_relation }}
        where dbt_valid_to is null

    ),

    insertions_source_data as (

        select
            *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_updated_at,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_valid_from,
            nullif({{ strategy.updated_at }}, {{ strategy.updated_at }}) as dbt_valid_to,
            {{ strategy.scd_id }} as dbt_scd_id

        from snapshot_query
    ),

    updates_source_data as (

        select
            *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_updated_at,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_valid_from,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_valid_to

        from snapshot_query
    ),

    {%- if strategy.invalidate_hard_deletes %}

    deletes_source_data as (

        select
            *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key
        from snapshot_query
    ),
    {% endif %}

    insertions as (

        select
            'insert' as dbt_change_type,
            source_data.*

        from insertions_source_data as source_data
        left outer join snapshotted_data on snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key = source_data.dbt_unique_key
        where snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key is null
           or (
                snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key is not null
            and (
                {{ strategy.row_changed }}
            )
        )

    ),

    updates as (

        select
            'update' as dbt_change_type,
            source_data.*,
            snapshotted_data.dbt_scd_id

        from updates_source_data as source_data
        join snapshotted_data on snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key = source_data.dbt_unique_key
        where (
            {{ strategy.row_changed }}
        )
    )

    {%- if strategy.invalidate_hard_deletes -%}
    ,

    deletes as (

        select
            'delete' as dbt_change_type,
            source_data.*,
            {{ snapshot_get_time() }} as dbt_valid_from,
            {{ snapshot_get_time() }} as dbt_updated_at,
            {{ snapshot_get_time() }} as dbt_valid_to,
            snapshotted_data.dbt_scd_id

        from snapshotted_data
        left join deletes_source_data as source_data on snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key = source_data.dbt_unique_key
        where source_data.dbt_unique_key is null
    )
    {%- endif %}

    select * from insertions
    union all
    select * from updates
    {%- if strategy.invalidate_hard_deletes %}
    union all
    select * from deletes
    {%- endif %}

{%- endmacro %}

macro.dbt.default__snapshot_merge_sql.name: default__snapshot_merge_sql
macro.dbt.default__snapshot_merge_sql.macro_sql:
{% macro default__snapshot_merge_sql(target, source, insert_cols) -%}
    {%- set insert_cols_csv = insert_cols | join(', ') -%}

    merge into {{ target }} as DBT_INTERNAL_DEST
    using {{ source }} as DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE
    on DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_scd_id = DBT_INTERNAL_DEST.dbt_scd_id

    when matched
     and DBT_INTERNAL_DEST.dbt_valid_to is null
     and DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_change_type in ('update', 'delete')
        then update
        set dbt_valid_to = DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_valid_to

    when not matched
     and DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_change_type = 'insert'
        then insert ({{ insert_cols_csv }})
        values ({{ insert_cols_csv }})

{% endmacro %}

{% macro default__get_date_dimension(start_date, end_date) %}
with base_dates as (
    {{ dbt_date.get_base_dates(start_date, end_date) }}
),
dates_with_prior_year_dates as (

    select
        cast(d.date_day as date) as date_day,
        cast({{ dbt_utils.dateadd('year', -1 , 'd.date_day') }} as date) as prior_year_date_day,
        cast({{ dbt_utils.dateadd('day', -364 , 'd.date_day') }} as date) as prior_year_over_year_date_day
    from
    	base_dates d

)
select
    d.date_day,
    {{ dbt_date.yesterday('d.date_day') }} as prior_date_day,
    {{ dbt_date.tomorrow('d.date_day') }} as next_date_day,
    d.prior_year_date_day as prior_year_date_day,
    d.prior_year_over_year_date_day,
    {{ dbt_date.day_of_week('d.date_day', isoweek=false) }} as day_of_week,
    {{ dbt_date.day_of_week('d.date_day', isoweek=true) }} as day_of_week_iso,
    {{ dbt_date.day_name('d.date_day', short=false) }} as day_of_week_name,
    {{ dbt_date.day_name('d.date_day', short=true) }} as day_of_week_name_short,
    {{ dbt_date.day_of_month('d.date_day') }} as day_of_month,
    {{ dbt_date.day_of_year('d.date_day') }} as day_of_year,

    {{ dbt_date.week_start('d.date_day') }} as week_start_date,
    {{ dbt_date.week_end('d.date_day') }} as week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.week_start('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_week_start_date,
    {{ dbt_date.week_end('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.week_of_year('d.date_day') }} as week_of_year,

    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_start('d.date_day') }} as iso_week_start_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_end('d.date_day') }} as iso_week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_start('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_iso_week_start_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_end('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_iso_week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_of_year('d.date_day') }} as iso_week_of_year,

    {{ dbt_date.week_of_year('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_week_of_year,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_of_year('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_iso_week_of_year,

    cast({{ dbt_date.date_part('month', 'd.date_day') }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as month_of_year,
    {{ dbt_date.month_name('d.date_day', short=false) }}  as month_name,
    {{ dbt_date.month_name('d.date_day', short=true) }}  as month_name_short,

    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('month', 'd.date_day') }} as date) as month_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.date_day', 'month') }} as date) as month_end_date,

    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('month', 'd.prior_year_date_day') }} as date) as prior_year_month_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.prior_year_date_day', 'month') }} as date) as prior_year_month_end_date,

    cast({{ dbt_date.date_part('quarter', 'd.date_day') }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as quarter_of_year,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('quarter', 'd.date_day') }} as date) as quarter_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.date_day', 'quarter') }} as date) as quarter_end_date,

    cast({{ dbt_date.date_part('year', 'd.date_day') }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as year_number,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('year', 'd.date_day') }} as date) as year_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.date_day', 'year') }} as date) as year_end_date
from
    dates_with_prior_year_dates d
order by 1
{% endmacro %}

    {{ dbt_date.week_end('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.week_of_year('d.date_day') }} as week_of_year,

    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_start('d.date_day') }} as iso_week_start_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_end('d.date_day') }} as iso_week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_start('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_iso_week_start_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_end('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_iso_week_end_date,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_of_year('d.date_day') }} as iso_week_of_year,

    {{ dbt_date.week_of_year('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_week_of_year,
    {{ dbt_date.iso_week_of_year('d.prior_year_over_year_date_day') }} as prior_year_iso_week_of_year,

    cast({{ dbt_date.date_part('month', 'd.date_day') }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as month_of_year,
    {{ dbt_date.month_name('d.date_day', short=false) }}  as month_name,
    {{ dbt_date.month_name('d.date_day', short=true) }}  as month_name_short,

    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('month', 'd.date_day') }} as date) as month_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.date_day', 'month') }} as date) as month_end_date,

    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('month', 'd.prior_year_date_day') }} as date) as prior_year_month_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.prior_year_date_day', 'month') }} as date) as prior_year_month_end_date,

    cast({{ dbt_date.date_part('quarter', 'd.date_day') }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as quarter_of_year,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('quarter', 'd.date_day') }} as date) as quarter_start_date,
    {# dbt_utils.last_day does not support quarter because postgresql does not support quarter interval. #}
    cast({{dbt_utils.dateadd('day', '-1', dbt_utils.dateadd('month', '3', dbt_utils.date_trunc('quarter', 'd.date_day')))}} as date) as quarter_end_date,

    cast({{ dbt_date.date_part('year', 'd.date_day') }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as year_number,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.date_trunc('year', 'd.date_day') }} as date) as year_start_date,
    cast({{ dbt_utils.last_day('d.date_day', 'year') }} as date) as year_end_date
from
    dates_with_prior_year_dates d
order by 1
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_date.get_fiscal_periods.name: get_fiscal_periods
macro.dbt_date.get_fiscal_periods.macro_sql:
{% macro get_fiscal_periods(dates, year_end_month, week_start_day, shift_year=1) %}
{# 
This macro requires you to pass in a ref to a date dimension, created via
dbt_date.get_date_dimension()s
#}
with fscl_year_dates_for_periods as (
    {{ dbt_date.get_fiscal_year_dates(dates, year_end_month, week_start_day, shift_year) }}
),
fscl_year_w13 as (

    select
        f.*,
        -- We count the weeks in a 13 week period
        -- and separate the 4-5-4 week sequences
        mod(cast(
            (f.fiscal_week_of_year-1) as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}
            ), 13) as w13_number,
        -- Chop weeks into 13 week merch quarters
        cast(
            least(
                floor((f.fiscal_week_of_year-1)/13.0)
                , 3)
            as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as quarter_number
    from
        fscl_year_dates_for_periods f

),
fscl_periods as (

    select
        f.date_day,
        f.fiscal_year_number,
        f.week_start_date,
        f.week_end_date,
        f.fiscal_week_of_year,
        case 
            -- we move week 53 into the 3rd period of the quarter
            when f.fiscal_week_of_year = 53 then 3
            when f.w13_number between 0 and 3 then 1
            when f.w13_number between 4 and 8 then 2
            when f.w13_number between 9 and 12 then 3
        end as period_of_quarter,
        f.quarter_number
    from
        fscl_year_w13 f

),
fscl_periods_quarters as (

    select
        f.*,
        cast((
            (f.quarter_number * 3) + f.period_of_quarter
         ) as {{ dbt_utils.type_int() }}) as fiscal_period_number
    from
        fscl_periods f

)
select
    date_day,
    fiscal_year_number,
    week_start_date,
    week_end_date,
    fiscal_week_of_year, 
    dense_rank() over(partition by fiscal_period_number order by fiscal_week_of_year) as fiscal_week_of_period,
    fiscal_period_number,
    quarter_number+1 as fiscal_quarter_number,
    period_of_quarter as fiscal_period_of_quarter
from 
    fscl_periods_quarters 
order by 1,2
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_date.get_fiscal_year_dates.name: get_fiscal_year_dates

macro.dbt_date.default__get_fiscal_year_.name: default__get_fiscal_year_dates
macro.dbt_date.default__get_fiscal_year_.macro_sql:
{% macro default__get_fiscal_year_dates(dates, year_end_month, week_start_day, shift_year) %}
-- this gets all the dates within a fiscal year
-- determined by the given year-end-month
-- ending on the saturday closest to that month's end date
with date_dimension as (
    select * from {{ dates }}
),
year_month_end as (

    select
       d.year_number - {{ shift_year }} as fiscal_year_number,
       d.month_end_date
    from
        date_dimension d
    where
        d.month_of_year = {{ year_end_month }}
    group by 1,2

),
weeks as (

    select
        d.year_number,
        d.month_of_year,
        d.date_day as week_start_date,
        cast({{ dbt_utils.dateadd('day', 6, 'd.date_day') }} as date) as week_end_date
    from
        date_dimension d
    where
        d.day_of_week = {{ week_start_day }}

),
-- get all the weeks that start in the month the year ends
year_week_ends as (

    select
        d.year_number - {{ shift_year }} as fiscal_year_number,
        d.week_end_date
    from
        weeks d
    where
        d.month_of_year = {{ year_end_month }}
    group by
        1,2

),
-- then calculate which Saturday is closest to month end
weeks_at_month_end as (

    select
        d.fiscal_year_number,
        d.week_end_date,
        m.month_end_date,
        rank() over
            (partition by d.fiscal_year_number
                order by
                abs({{ dbt_utils.datediff('d.week_end_date', 'm.month_end_date', 'day') }})

            ) as closest_to_month_end
    from
        year_week_ends d
        join
        year_month_end m on d.fiscal_year_number = m.fiscal_year_number
),
fiscal_year_range as (

    select
        w.fiscal_year_number,
        cast(
            {{ dbt_utils.dateadd('day', 1,
            'lag(w.week_end_date) over(order by w.week_end_date)') }}
            as date) as fiscal_year_start_date,
        w.week_end_date as fiscal_year_end_date
    from
        weeks_at_month_end w
    where
        w.closest_to_month_end = 1

),
fiscal_year_dates as (

    select
        d.date_day,
        m.fiscal_year_number,
        m.fiscal_year_start_date,
        m.fiscal_year_end_date,
        w.week_start_date,
        w.week_end_date,
        -- we reset the weeks of the year starting with the merch year start date
        dense_rank()
            over(
                partition by m.fiscal_year_number
                order by w.week_start_date
                ) as fiscal_week_of_year
    from
        date_dimension d
        join
        fiscal_year_range m on d.date_day between m.fiscal_year_start_date and m.fiscal_year_end_date
        join
        weeks w on d.date_day between w.week_start_date and w.week_end_date

)
select * from fiscal_year_dates order by 1
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_expectations.test_expect_colum.macro_sql:
{% test expect_column_values_to_have_consistent_casing(model, column_name, display_inconsistent_columns=False) %}

with test_data as (

    select
        distinct {{ column_name }} as distinct_values
    from
        {{ model }}

 ),
 {% if display_inconsistent_columns %}
 validation_errors as (

    select
        lower(distinct_values) as inconsistent_columns,
        count(distinct_values) as set_count_case_insensitive
    from
        test_data
    group by 1
    having
        count(distinct_values) > 1

 )
 select * from validation_errors
 {% else %}
 validation_errors as (

    select
        count(1) as set_count,
        count(distinct lower(distinct_values)) as set_count_case_insensitive
    from
        test_data

 )
 select *
 from
    validation_errors
 where
    set_count != set_count_case_insensitive
 {% endif %}
 {%- endtest -%}

macro.dbt_expectations.test_expect_colum.name: test_expect_column_values_to_be_within_n_moving_stdevs
macro.dbt_expectations.test_expect_colum.macro_sql:
{% test expect_column_values_to_be_within_n_moving_stdevs(model,
                                  column_name,
                                  date_column_name,
                                  period='day',
                                  lookback_periods=1,
                                  trend_periods=7,
                                  test_periods=14,
                                  sigma_threshold=3,
                                  sigma_threshold_upper=None,
                                  sigma_threshold_lower=None,
                                  take_diffs=true,
                                  take_logs=true
                                ) -%}
    {{ adapter.dispatch('test_expect_column_values_to_be_within_n_moving_stdevs', 'dbt_expectations') (model,
                                  column_name,
                                  date_column_name,
                                  period,
                                  lookback_periods,
                                  trend_periods,
                                  test_periods,
                                  sigma_threshold,
                                  sigma_threshold_upper,
                                  sigma_threshold_lower,
                                  take_diffs,
                                  take_logs
                                ) }}
{%- endtest %}

macro.dbt_expectations.default__test_exp.name: default__test_expect_column_values_to_be_within_n_moving_stdevs
macro.dbt_expectations.default__test_exp.macro_sql:
{% macro default__test_expect_column_values_to_be_within_n_moving_stdevs(model,
                                  column_name,
                                  date_column_name,
                                  period,
                                  lookback_periods,
                                  trend_periods,
                                  test_periods,
                                  sigma_threshold,
                                  sigma_threshold_upper,
                                  sigma_threshold_lower,
                                  take_diffs,
                                  take_logs
                                ) %}

{%- set sigma_threshold_upper = sigma_threshold_upper if sigma_threshold_upper else sigma_threshold -%}
{%- set sigma_threshold_lower = sigma_threshold_lower if sigma_threshold_lower else -1 * sigma_threshold -%}

with metric_values as (

    with grouped_metric_values as (

        select
            {{ dbt_utils.date_trunc(period, date_column_name) }} as metric_period,
            sum({{ column_name }}) as agg_metric_value
        from
            {{ model }}
        group by
            1

    )
    {%- if take_diffs %}
    , grouped_metric_values_with_priors as (

        select
            *,
            lag(agg_metric_value, {{ lookback_periods }}) over(order by metric_period) as prior_agg_metric_value
    from
        grouped_metric_values d

    )
    select
        *,
        {{ dbt_expectations._get_metric_expression("agg_metric_value", take_logs) }}
        -
        {{ dbt_expectations._get_metric_expression("prior_agg_metric_value", take_logs) }}
        as metric_test_value
    from
        grouped_metric_values_with_priors d

    {%- else %}

    select
        *,
        {{ dbt_expectations._get_metric_expression("agg_metric_value", take_logs) }}
        as metric_test_value
    from
        grouped_metric_values

    {%- endif %}

),
metric_moving_calcs as (

    select
        *,
        avg(metric_test_value)
            over(order by metric_period rows
                    between {{ trend_periods }} preceding and 1 preceding) as metric_test_rolling_average,
        stddev(metric_test_value)
            over(order by metric_period rows
                    between {{ trend_periods }} preceding and 1 preceding) as metric_test_rolling_stddev
    from
        metric_values

),
metric_sigma as (

    select
        *,
        (metric_test_value - metric_test_rolling_average) as metric_test_delta,
        (metric_test_value - metric_test_rolling_average)/nullif(metric_test_rolling_stddev, 0) as metric_test_sigma
    from
        metric_moving_calcs

)
select
    *
from
    metric_sigma
where

    metric_period >= cast(
            {{ dbt_utils.dateadd(period, -test_periods, dbt_utils.date_trunc(period, dbt_date.now())) }}
            as {{ dbt_utils.type_timestamp() }})
    and
    metric_period < {{ dbt_utils.date_trunc(period, dbt_date.now()) }}
    and

    not (
        metric_test_sigma >= {{ sigma_threshold_lower }} and
        metric_test_sigma <= {{ sigma_threshold_upper }}
    )
{%- endmacro -%}

macro.dbt_expectations.default__test_exp.name: default__test_expect_grouped_row_values_to_have_recent_data
macro.dbt_expectations.default__test_exp.macro_sql:
{% macro default__test_expect_grouped_row_values_to_have_recent_data(model,
                                                                        group_by,
                                                                        timestamp_column,
                                                                        datepart,
                                                                        interval,
                                                                        row_condition) %}
with latest_grouped_timestamps as (

    select
        {%- for g in group_by %}
        {{ g }},
        {%- endfor %}
        max(1) as join_key,
        max(cast({{ timestamp_column }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_timestamp() }})) as latest_timestamp_column
    from
        {{ model }}
    where
        -- to exclude erroneous future dates
        cast({{ timestamp_column }} as {{ dbt_utils.type_timestamp() }}) <= {{ dbt_date.now() }}
        {% if row_condition %}
        and {{ row_condition }}
        {% endif %}

    {{ dbt_utils.group_by(group_by | length )}}

),
total_row_counts as (

    select
        max(1) as join_key,
        count(*) as row_count
    from
        latest_grouped_timestamps

),
outdated_grouped_timestamps as (

    select *
    from
        latest_grouped_timestamps
    where
        -- are the max timestamps per group older than the specified cutoff?
        latest_timestamp_column <
            cast(
                {{ dbt_utils.dateadd(datepart, interval * -1, dbt_date.now()) }}
                as {{ dbt_utils.type_timestamp() }}
            )

),
validation_errors as (

    select
        r.row_count,
        t.*
    from
        total_row_counts r
        left join
        outdated_grouped_timestamps t
        on r.join_key = t.join_key
    where
        -- fail if either no rows were returned due to row_condition,
        -- or the recency test returned failed rows
        r.row_count = 0
        or
        t.join_key is not null

)
select * from validation_errors
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_expectations.default__test_exp.name: default__test_expect_table_row_count_to_equal

flow_models.play_type.name: play_type
flow_models.play_type.block_contents: The more granular (than play_category) type of play that is shown in the moment.

flow_models.player_stats_season_to_date.name: player_stats_season_to_date
flow_models.player_stats_season_to_date.block_contents: The season stats for the player as of the game featured in the moment.

flow_models.play_category.name: play_category
flow_models.play_category.block_contents: The broad category of play that is shown in the moment.

flow_models.swap_price.name: swap_price
flow_models.swap_price.block_contents: The asset price from a swap.

flow_models.contract_name.name: contract_name
flow_models.contract_name.block_contents: The primary name of the contract, derived from the full contract address.

flow_models.currency.name: currency
flow_models.currency.block_contents: The contract address refering to the currency used in the transaction. This may or may not relate to a token in the prices table, depending on the marketplace or transaction.

flow_models.teleport_contract.name: teleport_contract
flow_models.teleport_contract.block_contents: The contract address for the bridge used to move tokens to or from Flow.

flow_models.creator.name: creator
flow_models.creator.block_contents: The source of the labeling information for the address.

flow_models.token_contract.name: token_contract
flow_models.token_contract.block_contents: The contract address for a token on the Flow blockchain.

flow_models.token_2_amount.name: token_2_amount
flow_models.token_2_amount.block_contents: Amount being swapped for token 2 in the swap pair.

flow_models.decoded_address.name: decoded_address
flow_models.decoded_address.block_contents: The decoded address, if applicable, for the attribute_value.

flow_models.block_height.name: block_height
flow_models.block_height.block_contents: The block height the block was recorded at.

flow_models.source.name: source
flow_models.source.block_contents: Data source for the record in question. E.g. for the prices table, this may be an API or derived from swaps. Stableswap indicates the price was calculated from a swap where one of the assets was a stablecoin, while for a flowswap the other token was Flow.

flow_models.label_type.name: label_type
flow_models.label_type.block_contents: Predominant label categorization, such as CEX, Operator, NFT, etc.

flow_models.event_index.name: event_index
flow_models.event_index.block_contents: The index of the event within the transaction, i.e. in what order the events occurred.

flow_models.player.name: player
flow_models.player.block_contents: The player who is the focus of the moment.

flow_models.trader.name: trader
flow_models.trader.block_contents: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

flow_models.token_in_amount.name: token_in_amount
flow_models.token_in_amount.block_contents: The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).

flow_models.asset_id.name: asset_id
flow_models.asset_id.block_contents: Identifier used by the CoinMarketCap or CoinGecko APIs.

flow_models.buyer.name: buyer
flow_models.buyer.block_contents: The party in the transaction purchasing the asset, this is an account address.

flow_models.token_in_contract.name: token_in_contract
flow_models.token_in_contract.block_contents: The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.

flow_models.token_out_amount.name: token_out_amount
flow_models.token_out_amount.block_contents: The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).

flow_models.timestamp.name: timestamp
flow_models.timestamp.block_contents: Timestamp of when the data was recorded from the API or from on-chain data, depending on the source.

flow_models.parent_id.name: parent_id
flow_models.parent_id.block_contents: The block hash for the parent block.

flow_models.flow_wallet_address.name: flow_wallet_address
flow_models.flow_wallet_address.block_contents: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

flow_models.token_out_contract.name: token_out_contract
flow_models.token_out_contract.block_contents: The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.

flow_models.address_name.name: address_name
flow_models.address_name.block_contents: The name for a specific address, like Kraken or Huobi for CEX, or consensus vs verification for validator.

flow_models.set_name.name: set_name
flow_models.set_name.block_contents: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

flow_models.event_attributes.name: event_attributes
flow_models.event_attributes.block_contents: The data passed to the event, in the form of key-value pairs.

flow_models.symbol.name: symbol
flow_models.symbol.block_contents: Short-name symbol for the crypto token, e.g. BLT.

flow_models.total_circulation.name: total_circulation
flow_models.total_circulation.block_contents: The total number of moments minted for the particular play.

flow_models.payer.name: payer
flow_models.payer.block_contents: Address of the wallet paying for the transaction.

flow_models.listing_id.name: listing_id
flow_models.listing_id.block_contents: The listing ID used by the NFT marketplace.

flow_models.serial_number.name: serial_number
flow_models.serial_number.block_contents: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

flow_models.price.name: price
flow_models.price.block_contents: The price of the asset, in the currency specified in the table.

flow_models.amount_fee.name: amount_fee
flow_models.amount_fee.block_contents: The fee taken by the protocol or contract as part of the transaction.

flow_models._ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
flow_models._ingested_at.block_contents: When the record was ingested by the chainwalker.

flow_models.validator_type.name: validator_type
flow_models.validator_type.block_contents: The role of the node from one of the following options: collection, consensus, execution, verification, access. [See the Flow documentaion here for more.](https://docs.onflow.org/node-operation/node-roles/).

flow_models.direction.name: direction
flow_models.direction.block_contents: For bridging transactions, inbound refers to tokens bridged to the Flow blockchain, while outbound indicated tokens were sent from Flow to another (i.e. to Ethereum, BSC, etc.).

flow_models.recorded_at.name: recorded_at
flow_models.recorded_at.block_contents: Timestamp of when the data was recorded from the API or from on-chain data, depending on the source.

flow_models.tx_count.name: tx_count
flow_models.tx_count.block_contents: The number of transactions in the block.

flow_models.counterparties.name: counterparties
flow_models.counterparties.block_contents: An array containing the addresses interacted with during the tokenflow of the transaction. This contains all payers and recipients of tokens within the transaction.

flow_models.video_urls.name: video_urls
flow_models.video_urls.block_contents: An array containing links to the moments.

flow_models.marketplace.name: marketplace
flow_models.marketplace.block_contents: Contract address for the marketplace where the transaction occurred. Flow uses a general-purpose contract at `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront` for a significant number of sales. This column does not necessarily indicate the website or specific platform on which the sale occurred.

flow_models.season.name: season
flow_models.season.block_contents: The season during which the moment occurred.

flow_models.event_data.name: event_data
flow_models.event_data.block_contents: The raw event data from the event.

flow_models.gas_limit.name: gas_limit
flow_models.gas_limit.block_contents: Upper gas limit attached to the transaction.

flow_models._inserted_timestamp.name: _inserted_timestamp
flow_models._inserted_timestamp.block_contents: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

flow_models.net_amount.name: net_amount
flow_models.net_amount.block_contents: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction, net of any fees or royalties.

flow_models.project_name.name: project_name
flow_models.project_name.block_contents:
The overarching project name, related to the address name. For example, Kraken (vs. Kraken Deposit Wallet for address_names).
For validators, this will be the name only if provided as most validators do not provide such a name.

flow_models.attribute_index.name: attribute_index
flow_models.attribute_index.block_contents: Index of the attribute within the event, corresponds with how the attributes are presented on the explorer.

flow_models.step_action.name: step_action
flow_models.step_action.block_contents: An array containing the actions taken at index `n` within the tokenflow.

flow_models.attribute_value_adj.name: attribute_value_adj
flow_models.attribute_value_adj.block_contents: The adjusted attribute_value where an address from decoded_address is used in place of the encoded binary string, where applicable.

flow_models.event_type.name: event_type
flow_models.event_type.block_contents: The type of method called on the event_contract. This is equivalent to the Type column on Flowscan.

flow_models.team.name: team
flow_models.team.block_contents: The team that the player from the moment is on, at the time of the moment.

flow_models.transaction_result.name: transaction_result
flow_models.transaction_result.block_contents: Outcome of the transaction, including events that were called.

flow_models.label_subtype.name: label_subtype
flow_models.label_subtype.block_contents: The type of address, within the higher order label_type, such as hot wallet, deposit wallet, validator, etc.

flow_models.account_address.name: account_address
flow_models.account_address.block_contents: The account address in 0x form that instantiated the contract called in the event. Note, on the Flow blockchain the account and the contract are separate as the account may have created more than one contract, in rare instances, such as the following FLOW address 0x8624b52f9ddcd04a.

flow_models.storefront_id.name: storefront_id
flow_models.storefront_id.block_contents: The ID for the NFT storefront, from the listing event.

flow_models.nbatopshot_id.name: nbatopshot_id
flow_models.nbatopshot_id.block_contents: The ID used by NBA TopShot. This can be pasted into the URL https://nbatopshot.com/moment/{nbatopshot_id} to view the moment on the marketplace.

flow_models.bridge_contract.name: bridge_contract
flow_models.bridge_contract.block_contents: The contract address for the bridge used to move tokens to or from Flow.

flow_models.attribute_value.name: attribute_value
flow_models.attribute_value.block_contents: The value of the attribute key-value pair. This is the data that attribute_key labels.

flow_models.amount.name: amount
flow_models.amount.block_contents: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction.

flow_models.event_contract.name: event_contract
flow_models.event_contract.block_contents: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

flow_models.network.name: network
flow_models.network.block_contents: The blockchain network the block or transaction occurred on.

flow_models.blockchain.name: blockchain
flow_models.blockchain.block_contents: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

flow_models.swap_side.name: swap_side
flow_models.swap_side.block_contents: The side of the pool that the trader is engaging, per the Trade or Swap event_data.

flow_models.teleport_direction.name: teleport_direction
flow_models.teleport_direction.block_contents: For bridging transactions, inbound refers to tokens bridged to the Flow blockchain, while outbound indicated tokens were sent from Flow to another (i.e. to Ethereum, BSC, etc.).

flow_models.nft_id.name: nft_id
flow_models.nft_id.block_contents: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

flow_models.gross_amount.name: gross_amount
flow_models.gross_amount.block_contents: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction, before any fees or royalities.

flow_models.event_data_listing.name: event_data_listing
flow_models.event_data_listing.block_contents: The raw event data from the ListingCompleted event.

flow_models.tx_succeeded.name: tx_succeeded
flow_models.tx_succeeded.block_contents: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

flow_models.player_stats_game.name: player_stats_game
flow_models.player_stats_game.block_contents: The statline for the player in the Moment, from the game highlighted.

flow_models.swap_contract.name: swap_contract
flow_models.swap_contract.block_contents: The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.

flow_models.market_cap.name: market_cap
flow_models.market_cap.block_contents: Total market cap of the token, where available, in USD.

flow_models.event_id.name: event_id
flow_models.event_id.block_contents: The id for the event, which is a concatenation of tx_id-event_index.

flow_models._event_data_fields.name: _event_data_fields
flow_models._event_data_fields.block_contents: The fields object from the event_data.

flow_models.seller.name: seller
flow_models.seller.block_contents: The party in the transaction selling the asset, this is an account address.

flow_models.token_1_amount.name: token_1_amount
flow_models.token_1_amount.block_contents: Amount being swapped for token 1 in the swap pair.

flow_models.step_data.name: step_data
flow_models.step_data.block_contents: An array containing the data passed at index `n` within the tokenflow.

flow_models.nft_collection.name: nft_collection
flow_models.nft_collection.block_contents: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

flow_models.bridge.name: bridge
flow_models.bridge.block_contents: The name of the bridge or protocol used.

flow_models.block_timestamp.name: block_timestamp
flow_models.block_timestamp.block_contents: The date and time for when the block was written.

flow_models.moment_stats_full.name: moment_stats_full
flow_models.moment_stats_full.block_contents: A dictionary of the full moment stats.

flow_models.attribute_key.name: attribute_key
flow_models.attribute_key.block_contents: The key of the attribute key-value pair. This is the label while attribute_value is the data.

flow_models.token.name: token
flow_models.token.block_contents: Friendly name of the crypto token, e.g. Blocto Token.

flow_models.chain_id.name: chain_id
flow_models.chain_id.block_contents: The id for the chain of the network on which this block occurred.

flow_models.attribute_id.name: attribute_id
flow_models.attribute_id.block_contents: ID for the attribute, which is a concatenation of tx_id-event_index-attribute_index.

flow_models.tokenflow.name: tokenflow
flow_models.tokenflow.block_contents: An array of events tracking the flow of tokens, in event_index order, for a transaction. This is constructed from the following events within a transaction: `TokensDeposited`, `TokensWithdrawn`, `ForwardedDeposit`.

flow_models.node_id.name: node_id
flow_models.node_id.block_contents: The address, known on the Flow blockchain as Node ID, of the validator.

flow_models.num_steps.name: num_steps
flow_models.num_steps.block_contents: The number of steps (events) taken by tokens within the transaction. This includes only tokenflow steps, and is not the count of total events within the transaction.

flow_models.moment_description.name: moment_description
flow_models.moment_description.block_contents: Long-form description of the play, from NBA TopShot.

flow_models.counterparty.name: counterparty
flow_models.counterparty.block_contents:
The alternate address involved in this transaction, if available.  
For bridge transactions, this may be the address on another blockchain.

flow_models.set_series_number.name: set_series_number
flow_models.set_series_number.block_contents: The series number for this particular set of drops, within the set_name.

dbt.__overview__.name: __overview__
dbt.__overview__.block_contents:
### Welcome!

Welcome to the auto-generated documentation for your dbt project!

### Navigation

You can use the `Project` and `Database` navigation tabs on the left side of the window to explore the models
in your project.

#### Project Tab
The `Project` tab mirrors the directory structure of your dbt project. In this tab, you can see all of the
models defined in your dbt project, as well as models imported from dbt packages.

#### Database Tab
The `Database` tab also exposes your models, but in a format that looks more like a database explorer. This view
shows relations (tables and views) grouped into database schemas. Note that ephemeral models are _not_ shown
in this interface, as they do not exist in the database.

### Graph Exploration
You can click the blue icon on the bottom-right corner of the page to view the lineage graph of your models.

On model pages, you'll see the immediate parents and children of the model you're exploring. By clicking the `Expand`
button at the top-right of this lineage pane, you'll be able to see all of the models that are used to build,
or are built from, the model you're exploring.

Once expanded, you'll be able to use the `--select` and `--exclude` model selection syntax to filter the
models in the graph. For more information on model selection, check out the [dbt docs](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/model-selection-syntax).

Note that you can also right-click on models to interactively filter and explore the graph.

---

### More information

- [What is dbt](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/introduction)?
- Read the [dbt viewpoint](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/viewpoint)
- [Installation](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/installation)
- Join the [dbt Community](https://www.getdbt.com/community/) for questions and discussion
